title: I Made $14,870 Trading NQ in 5 Weeks Using One Repeating Setup Most Traders Ignore
author: ACE-612
selftext:
No indicators.

No Discord alerts.

No “AI strategy.”

Just the same liquidity setup repeated over and over.

Started these prop accounts with about $420 in eval fees total. Current payouts across 3 funded accounts are sitting at $14,870 after withdrawals.

What’s funny is I spent years trying to predict direction before realizing the market mostly just moves from one pool of liquidity to another.

The Setup: Liquidity Sweep → Displacement → Retracement → Expansion

That’s basically it.

Most traders get chopped up because they enter in the middle of ranges. I wait for price to first run an obvious high or low where stops are sitting.

Previous day high.

Previous day low.

Equal highs.

Asia session high.

Weekly low.

Once liquidity gets taken, I’m watching for aggressive displacement in the opposite direction. That move usually leaves behind a fair value gap or imbalance on the 15m or 30m.

That retracement into the imbalance is where I enter.

Simple example:

Price sweeps previous day low during NY session.

Sellers pile in late.

Price aggressively reclaims the range and leaves a clean FVG behind.

I wait for retracement into that gap.

Target becomes opposing liquidity above the range.

That single sequence makes up most of my profitable trades.

The Stats From My Journal (last 2 months)

47 trades total

31 wins / 16 losses

65.9% win rate

2.84 profit factor

Average winner: $512

Average loser: $289

Most trades are done within 1-3 hours. I’m not sitting at the charts all day anymore trying to force action.

The Weekly Pattern I Keep Seeing

Monday usually sets the weekly range.

Tuesday or Wednesday often sweeps one side of that range before the real move starts.

Once that sweep happens, I stop fighting the direction and only look for continuation setups with the higher timeframe bias.

That alone removed a ridiculous amount of bad trades from my trading.

Risk Management

1% risk per trade

2 trades max per day

If first trade wins, I usually stop

No revenge trading

No adding to losers

Move stop to breakeven once structure confirms

Honestly the strategy itself isn’t even the hard part anymore.

The hard part is sitting there doing absolutely nothing while price slowly approaches your level without convincing yourself to enter early.

Most of my improvement came from trading less, not more.

comments.author: theRealDamnpenguins
comments.body:
Sorry mate, assuming this is real, congrats ;) 

But as soon as I read "fvg" my eyes glaze over and I find myself lacking the will to live.... 

I'm always amazed at how much this guy's disciples are making on social media when their deity couldn't trade his way out of a wet paper bag.... 

Trading reactions to levels of liquidity is timeless. What's priceless is convincing people that you invented it....
